global_cues.us_markets: Friday May 8 close strong: S&P 500 +0.8% to 7,399 (fresh record), Nasdaq +1.7% to 26,247 (record), Dow +12 pts to 49,609. Rally driven by stronger-than-expected April nonfarm payrolls (+115k vs est., unemployment 4.3%) which overshadowed renewed US-Iran military exchange.
global_cues.european_markets: European indices broadly firm into the weekend, tracking US strength; sentiment moderated by Mideast/oil concerns.
global_cues.asian_markets: Asia-Pacific mostly positive in Monday morning trade, shrugging off Middle East tensions and tracking Wall Street records. GIFT Nifty hovering near 24,218-24,280 (~0.2-0.4% above Friday Nifty 50 close of 24,176), pointing to a modest gap-up open for Indian equities.

fii_dii_summary: FIIs remain in heavy selling mode — net sellers of Rs 68,870 crore in April 2026 and selling pressure has extended into May (CYTD outflows ~Rs 2.06 lakh crore). DIIs continue to absorb the supply, having infused over Rs 3 lakh crore YTD, providing a structural floor. Net stance into today: bearish FII, supportive DII — index direction will hinge on whether DII bids can keep pace with persistent FII outflows.
earnings_calendar: INDHOTEL (Indian Hotels Co - IHCL) - May 11 (Q4 & FY26; global conference call 7:00 PM IST)
macro_events: India April CPI inflation print scheduled May 12 (March was 3.4%; FY26 RBI projection 4.5%) — key for rate-cut path
India March IIP / new IIP base-year (2022-23) series release expected in May 2026
RBI 4% inflation target with 2-6% tolerance band reaffirmed for FY27-FY31 (April 2026 to March 2031)
RBI policy stance in 'withdrawal of accommodation' mode with focus on returning inflation to target while supporting growth
US April jobs report (May 8) printed above estimates, supports soft-landing narrative for global risk assets
sector_themes: IT outperforming as defensive/INR-hedge play: Nifty IT was top sectoral gainer (+1.2%) Friday — watch TCS, INFY, HCLTECH, WIPRO, TECHM, LTIM on continued buying
Pharma showing relative resilience and benefits from weaker INR for exports: DRREDDY and CIPLA earnings May 12 are catalysts; also watch SUNPHARMA, LUPIN, CIPLA, AUROPHARMA
Banking/Financials under pressure: Nifty PSU Bank -3.06% and Financial Services -1.66% Friday — watch SBIN, PNB, BANKBARODA, CANBK, UNIONBANK; private peers HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, AXISBANK, KOTAKBANK
Auto sector weak on elevated fuel costs hurting demand: MARUTI, TATAMOTORS (results May 14), M&M, BAJAJ-AUTO, HEROMOTOCO, EICHERMOT in focus
Oil & Gas / OMCs squeezed by Brent near $101: BPCL (results May 12) and HINDPETRO (May 13) margins at risk; upstream RELIANCE, ONGC, OIL relatively positioned
Hospitality / Travel: INDHOTEL Q4 today — read-across to LEMONTREE, CHALET, EIHOTEL
Telecom: BHARTIARTL results May 13 — also watch IDEA, RJIO (via RELIANCE)
Metals: JSWSTEEL May 13, HINDALCO May 22 — watch TATASTEEL, JINDALSTEL, SAIL, VEDL on global cues
risk_flags: Brent crude near $101/bbl on renewed US-Iran military exchange and Strait of Hormuz disruption concerns — every $10 sustained rise hurts India's CAD, fiscal math and OMCs (BPCL, HINDPETRO, IOC)
USD/INR around 93 — rupee at the weaker end of recent range, imported inflation risk and headwind for crude-importers, autos, paints; tailwind for IT and pharma exporters
Persistent FII outflows (~Rs 2.06 lakh crore CYTD) — sustained selling could overwhelm DII buying if global risk-off resumes

India-Pakistan tensions remain elevated one year after Operation Sindoor — escalatory threshold has lowered per think-tank assessments; tail risk for defence sector (HAL results May 13, BEL, BDL) and broader sentiment
Middle East / US-Iran flare-up risk — direct channel via crude and gold; geopolitical premium not yet fully priced
Mid-month CPI print on May 12 — any upside surprise above 4% could derail rate-cut hopes and pressure rate-sensitives
brief_summary: Indian markets head into Monday with mixed cues — strong US records (S&P 7,399, Nasdaq 26,247) and a modestly positive GIFT Nifty point to a gap-up, but Friday's domestic close was weak (Nifty -0.62% to 24,176) on banking/auto selling, Brent near $101, and continued FII outflows. Today is light on heavyweight earnings (INDHOTEL post-market the main event), so positioning ahead of Tuesday's CPI print and the BPCL/DRREDDY/CIPLA/TATAPOWER cluster will dominate flows. Bias: defensive (IT, pharma) over cyclicals (PSU banks, autos, OMCs) until oil cools or CPI confirms disinflation.
